sections.title: Revision of Days 21-30 — Introduction and Formula
sections.hindiTitle: Revision of Days 21-30 — परिचय और फॉर्मूला
sections.explanation: Revision of Days 21-30 is an essential building block of English grammar. Formula: Mixed revision: Used To, Could, Should Have, Must Have, Could Have, Would Have, May Have, Might Have, Will/Shall, Would+Ought To+Dare. Rules: Used to = past habit, Could = past ability | Should have / Must have / Could have / Would have / May have / Might have = perfect modals + V3, each shows a different level of certainty or regret | Will/Shall = future action, Ought to = moral duty, Dare = courage
sections.formula: Mixed revision: Used To, Could, Should Have, Must Have, Could Have, Would Have, May Have, Might Have, Will/Shall, Would+Ought To+Dare

examples.hindi: मैं रोज़ स्कूल जाता था।
examples.english: I used to go to school daily.

examples.hindi: मैं बचपन में तैर सकता था।
examples.english: I could swim when I was a child.

examples.hindi: मुझे पहले आना चाहिए था।
examples.english: I should have come earlier.

examples.hindi: उसने जरूर बहुत मेहनत की होगी।
examples.english: He must have worked very hard.

examples.hindi: मैं यह पहले कर सकता था।
examples.english: I could have done this earlier.

examples.hindi: अगर मुझे पता होता, तो मैं आता।
examples.english: If I had known, I would have come.

sections.title: Revision of Days 21-30 — Daily Life Usage
sections.hindiTitle: Revision of Days 21-30 — रोज़मर्रा के उपयोग
sections.explanation: Everyday sentences using Revision of Days 21-30 for daily conversation.
sections.formula: Mixed revision: Used To, Could, Should Have, Must Have, Could Have, Would Have, May Have, Might Have, Will/Shall, Would+Ought To+Dare

examples.hindi: वह शायद वहाँ गया होगा।
examples.english: He may have gone there.

examples.hindi: वह शायद गया होगा, लेकिन पक्का नहीं।
examples.english: He might have gone, but I am not sure.

examples.hindi: मैं कल आऊँगा।
examples.english: I will come tomorrow.

examples.hindi: हमें अपने माता-पिता की इज्जत करनी चाहिए।
examples.english: We ought to respect our parents.

examples.hindi: क्या तुम मुझे मदद कर सकते हो?
examples.english: Could you help me?

examples.hindi: तुम्हें उससे माफी मांगनी चाहिए थी।
examples.english: You should have apologized to him.

sections.title: Revision of Days 21-30 — Office & Professional Usage
sections.hindiTitle: Revision of Days 21-30 — ऑफिस और पेशेवर उपयोग
sections.explanation: Practice Revision of Days 21-30 in professional and office contexts.
sections.formula: Mixed revision: Used To, Could, Should Have, Must Have, Could Have, Would Have, May Have, Might Have, Will/Shall, Would+Ought To+Dare

examples.hindi: उसने ऐसा कहने की हिम्मत की।
examples.english: He dared to say this.

examples.hindi: क्या मैं दरवाज़ा बंद कर दूँ?
examples.english: Shall I close the door?

examples.hindi: वह पहले सिगरेट पीता था।
examples.english: He used to smoke.

examples.hindi: शायद उसने यह किताब पढ़ी होगी।
examples.english: She may have read this book.

sections.title: Revision of Days 21-30 — Negative and Question Forms
sections.hindiTitle: Revision of Days 21-30 — नकारात्मक और प्रश्न रूप
sections.explanation: How to make negative sentences and questions using Revision of Days 21-30. Common mistakes are highlighted here.
sections.formula: Mixed revision: Used To, Could, Should Have, Must Have, Could Have, Would Have, May Have, Might Have, Will/Shall, Would+Ought To+Dare

examples.hindi: तुम मुझे बता सकते थे।
examples.english: You could have told me.

examples.hindi: हमें नियमों का पालन करना चाहिए।
examples.english: We ought to follow the rules.

examples.hindi: शायद उसने यह सुना होगा।
examples.english: She might have heard this.

examples.hindi: हम कल मिलेंगे।
examples.english: We will meet tomorrow.
